tagline: Filipino coffee, pulled slow on Sunset.

hero.heading: Filipino coffee. Pulled slow. Poured warm.
hero.subheading: Single-origin Cordillera beans, ube ice cream churned in-house, and a Sunset Boulevard corner that runs on hand-built drinks.

about.label: Our story
about.heading: "Malaya" means free.
paragraphs: We named the shop after the Tagalog word for free — because that's how good coffee should feel. No production-line lattes, no scripted upsells, no rush. Just the bean, the water, and the hands.

Our beans come from the Cordilleras and Sagada, hand-picked and lot-roasted weekly so the cup you drink today was green a week ago. The ube ice cream and pandan affogato are made in the back, by us, in small batches.
Come read a book on the bench. Linger over your second pour. We're not a queue clock.

story.lead: When we opened Malaya in 2023, the goal was simple: bring the coffee we grew up with to the people who hadn't tried it yet. Cordillera beans pulled the way our titos and titas drink them — strong, slow, and shared.
paragraphs: Filipino coffee has a different lineage than the Italian-inflected lattes you find on most LA corners. The Cordilleras' Arabicas grow at elevation, harvested by smallholder farms whose families have been picking these cherries for generations. We pay flat-rate, direct-trade, and we name the farm on every bag.
The ice cream came later, when a regular asked if we could pair an espresso with ube. We churned a batch by hand the next morning, and it never came off the menu.

services.title: Cordillera Pour-Over
services.description: Single-origin Arabica from Sagada and Benguet. Bright, floral, gentle finish. Brewed to order.

services.title: Kapeng Barako
services.description: The deep, smoky liberica varietal that built Filipino coffee. Served black, no apologies.

services.title: Ube Latte
services.description: Double espresso with real purple yam paste and oat milk. Earthy, slightly sweet, photogenic.

services.title: Pandan Affogato
services.description: House-churned pandan ice cream drowned in a fresh shot of espresso. The reason regulars come back.

services.title: Ube Halo-Halo Ice Cream
services.description: Hand-churned ube with sweet beans, jackfruit, and toasted rice. One scoop, two, or a flight of three.

services.title: Whole Bean — 12 oz
services.description: Take Sagada or Benguet home. Roasted the same week, ground to your method on the spot.

reviews.name: Issa M.
reviews.text: Best Filipino coffee in LA, full stop. The Sagada pour-over is delicate and clean, the ube latte tastes like an actual yam, and the staff actually wants to talk to you about what you're drinking.

reviews.name: Marcus K.
reviews.text: I came in for an oat latte and left with a bag of beans, a pandan affogato, and a recommendation for the best lumpia in Echo Park. This is what a neighborhood coffee shop is supposed to feel like.

reviews.name: Nadia P.
reviews.text: Stumbled in on a walk and I'm so glad. The ube halo-halo ice cream was unbelievable — the kind of thing that makes you text three friends before you've finished it.

contact.mapEmbedQuery: Malaya Coffee, 2839 Sunset Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90026

seo.title: Malaya Coffee — Filipino-rooted coffee & ube ice cream in Silverlake
seo.description: Single-origin Cordillera coffee, ube lattes, and house-churned pandan ice cream on Sunset Boulevard. A Filipino-rooted Silverlake coffee shop, 4.8/5 on Google.

typography.mood: claymorphism, rounded, tactile, soft, warm, hand-craft, story-driven

moves: Claymorphic dual-shadow surfaces (light TL + warm BR) on cream — extruded buttons and inset panels
Rounded pill nav floating with sticky offset — clay button language throughout
Storytelling split hero with photo inside soft clay frame and drift animation
Hand-written Caveat script for warm eyebrows ('Hello, Silverlake —', '✱ Our story')
Drop-cap editorial story block with multi-paragraph Filipino-coffee narrative
Menu items as soft clay tiles with numbered chips (01/02/03) and right-aligned terracotta prices — NOT spec lists
Asymmetric staggered review bubbles with oversized open-quote glyph on the corner
Visit block as one big clay card containing an inset map panel — not split columns
Full-bleed terracotta-gradient CTA bean with cream pill button
